why_line: Trump taps Wall Street's Jay Clayton for DNI and Warner's endorsement signals a fast confirmation — a nomination, not yet a swearing-in, so it scores as the move it is.
Zuckerberg's months-old 6,500-engineer AI unit is reportedly a 'soul-crushing gulag' on the verge of revolt — a recruiting gift to every rival, stacked on top of the forced Manus unwind.
Washington unilaterally pulled the plug on Anthropic's most powerful models — the first time the government has recalled a deployed commercial AI, and a precedent that hangs over Amodei's entire IPO.
Beijing forces Zuckerberg to tear apart a $2B done deal — the first reversal of a completed US-China AI acquisition, and a sign the entire cross-border corridor just slammed shut.
State AGs open a coordinated probe into OpenAI's ad and health-data practices just as Altman races to file — the tobacco playbook arrives at the worst possible moment for the IPO.
Hegseth brags the Pentagon kicked Anthropic out 'forever' three months ago — and Mark Kelly's backing makes the pile-on bipartisan, the signal Amodei should fear most.

Microsoft cuts its own employees off from Claude Fable 5 — Nadella, both competitor and customer, joins the pile-on as Anthropic's enterprise base starts to wobble in the same week Washington moved against it.
OpenAI and Anthropic staff have quietly pulled $14B off the table before the IPOs — the insiders cashing out while public-market enthusiasm climbs is the tell Altman can't spin.

Trump declares an Iran deal done — but it isn't signed until Friday and Graham, Reed, Jeffries and Pence are all reading from different scripts; an announcement, not a consummation, so it scores as the claim it is.

Washington reframes the Anthropic takedown as a deliberate export-control weapon, not a jailbreak scare — Trump turns the first-ever frontier-model recall into a precedent that shadows Amodei's entire IPO.
The market does the math the export order set up: every dollar Anthropic loses abroad reroutes to Zhipu, whose 33% pop is a price tag on Amodei's foreign book.
London locks under-16s out of the feed and the regulatory dominoes are now global — another structural cut into Meta's user funnel in a week Zuckerberg can least afford it.
Inflation punches through 4% on Iran energy just as Warsh settles into the chair — the new Fed boss inherits a fire on day one, and the number is the receipt.
Blanche's DOJ drops indictments on four shippers and seven executives — the acting AG showing teeth on enforcement while his own confirmation is still in motion.
Thune strips Trump's voter-ID demand off the FISA bill and dares him to fight it — the Senate leader publicly choosing his own chamber over the President's wish list.
Blanche walks into Grassley's office to start the confirmation clock — the AG nominee one vote from a board seat takes his first formal step toward it.
